writ.title: Cartograph: collapse companion docs into `writ.ext['cartograph']`
writ.body:
# Cartograph: collapse companion docs into `writ.ext['cartograph']`

## Intent

The Cartograph apparatus today maintains three companion books — `cartograph/visions`, `cartograph/charges`, `cartograph/pieces` — each holding a `{id, stage, codex, createdAt, updatedAt}` document keyed one-for-one to a writ id. This pattern predates the writ-level `ext` slot. Now that `writ.ext` exists as a sanctioned plugin-keyed metadata slot with a transactional read-modify-write API, those companion books carry pure metadata that can live directly on the writ row.

This commission collapses the three companion books into a single `writ.ext['cartograph'] = { stage, codex }` sub-slot. The cartograph's typed-API surface stays — every existing callable continues to work with the same arguments and return shapes — but the internals shed three Stacks books, three companion-doc shapes, and the dual-write transactional ceremony around them. `createdAt` / `updatedAt` were always duplicates of the writ's own timestamps; they go away.

## Motivation

- **The companion-doc pattern is the pre-`ext` workaround for plugin-owned writ metadata.** Now that `ext` exists as the sanctioned slot, a pure-metadata companion book is exactly what `ext` was added to replace.
- **The substrate work that depends on the cartograph (the surveyor-apparatus, see Commission C in the surveying-cascade design subtree) is best designed against the post-cleanup shape.** Building the substrate's CDC observer, completion-stamping logic, and rig routing on top of the current companion-doc pattern, then reshaping it later, is strictly more work than landing the cleanup first. The substrate's CDC observer becomes a writ-type-filtered subscription (a primitive Stacks already supports cleanly) rather than a per-companion-book subscription.
- **No external behavioral change is intended.** Every consumer (CLI tools, tests, the apply CLI shipped in Commission A) reads the same shape from the same API. The cleanup is internal.

## Non-negotiable decisions

### 1. `ext['cartograph']` is the canonical home for stage and codex

```typescript
interface CartographExt {
  stage: VisionStage | ChargeStage | PieceStage;  // type depends on the writ type
  codex?: string;
}
```

The plugin id is `cartograph`. The slot is plugin-keyed under the established convention; the cartograph apparatus is the only writer.

### 2. The typed-API surface stays stable

`CartographApi` keeps every method it has today:

- `createVision` / `createCharge` / `createPiece`
- `showVision` / `showCharge` / `showPiece`
- `listVisions` / `listCharges` / `listPieces`
- `patchVision` / `patchCharge` / `patchPiece`
- `transitionVision` / `transitionCharge` / `transitionPiece`

Argument shapes (`CreateVisionRequest`, `TransitionChargeRequest`, etc.) and return shapes (`VisionDoc`, `ChargeDoc`, `PieceDoc`) stay. The doc shapes are projections over `writ.ext['cartograph']` rather than rows in a separate book — but from the caller's view, nothing changes.

`createdAt` and `updatedAt` on the projection map to the writ's own timestamps.

### 3. Atomicity for create + transition

`clerk.transition` silently strips `ext` from its body (per the existing Clerk contract). Any operation that touches both `writ.phase` and `writ.ext['cartograph']` MUST wrap the `clerk.transition` and `clerk.setWritExt` calls in a single `stacks.transaction` so the pair is atomic. This applies to `createX` (post + setWritExt) and `transitionX` (transition + setWritExt) alike.

The existing `createVision({ stage })` parameter — which Commission A relied on for the single-event-per-apply guarantee — keeps its semantic: the cartograph writes the writ at its initial phase, stamps `ext['cartograph']` with the supplied stage, and (if the stage isn't the writ-type's initial state) transitions to it, all inside one `stacks.transaction`.

### 4. Read-side projection

`showVision(id)` / `listVisions(filters)` continue to return `VisionDoc`-shaped values. Internally they:

- read the writ via `clerk.show(id)` (or `clerk.list({ type: 'vision' })`)
- project `writ.ext['cartograph'].stage` and `writ.ext['cartograph'].codex` onto the returned shape
- carry the writ's `createdAt` and `updatedAt` directly

The existing `VisionDoc` / `ChargeDoc` / `PieceDoc` exported types stay as projection shapes (their definitions can be retained verbatim — the only difference is where the data comes from). Filtering on `stage` becomes a JSON-field filter against `writ.ext['cartograph'].stage`; Stacks' query layer supports this.

### 5. Companion-book deletion

`cartograph/visions`, `cartograph/charges`, and `cartograph/pieces` Stacks books are removed entirely. There is no migration of historical companion-doc rows — the cartograph is recently-shipped and nothing in production-style guild data depends on those books. Any test fixtures or integration tests that opened the companion books directly should be updated to read via the typed API.

### 6. Surveying-cascade arch doc updates

The settled architecture for the surveying cascade (`docs/architecture/surveying-cascade.md`) names the companion-doc pattern explicitly in three sections. Update them to reflect the post-cleanup shape:

- **§3.4 — Companion `SurveyDoc` holds envelope metadata only.** SurveyDoc was modelled on the cartograph companion-doc pattern. Rewrite this section so the survey-writ envelope metadata (rigVersion, surveyorId, completedAt — minus targetNodeId, which is already `writ.parentId`, and minus rigName, which is already `writ.type`) lives in `status['surveyor']` / `ext['surveyor']` sub-slots on the survey writ rather than in a `books.surveys` companion book. Note that `completedAt` is redundant with `writ.resolvedAt`. The substrate is the only writer.

- **§3.6 — Substrate watches cartograph CDC.** The current text lists three book-event streams (`book.cartograph.visions.{created,updated}`, `book.cartograph.charges.{created,updated}`, `book.cartograph.pieces.{created,updated}`). Update to a single subscription on the writs book filtered by `writ.type ∈ {vision, charge, piece}`. The single-event-per-apply guarantee discussion stays — it shifts from "wrap createVision + transition in one transaction" to "the cartograph's createX/transitionX primitives are already transactional" with the same outcome (one CDC-significant phase transition per apply).

- **§3.7 — Substrate plugin shape.** The substrate-owned-things list includes "Owns survey writ types and `books.surveys`." Drop `books.surveys` from the list. The substrate now owns the `status['surveyor']` and `ext['surveyor']` slots on survey writs instead. Likewise drop "Stamps SurveyDoc on completion" and replace with "Stamps `status['surveyor']` on completion."

These three sections are the only load-bearing references; minor incidental references elsewhere in the doc may need to be touched up for consistency.

## Out of scope

- **Migrating SurveyDoc itself.** The surveyor-apparatus doesn't exist yet (Commission C in the surveying-cascade design subtree). This commission updates the arch doc to reflect the post-cleanup shape so Commission C is briefed against it, but does not build the substrate.
- **Migrating other companion-doc patterns elsewhere in the framework.** The Astrolabe `PlanDoc` and similar are out of scope; PlanDoc is mostly content (inventory, scope, decisions, spec) and isn't a metadata-only companion.
- **Reworking the `ext` API itself.** `clerk.setWritExt` and the slot-write contract are existing primitives; this commission consumes them, doesn't extend them.
- **Renaming `VisionDoc` / `ChargeDoc` / `PieceDoc` projection types.** They keep their names and shapes — only their backing storage moves.

## References

- Parent design subtree: `c-moji050w` — Cartograph + surveying cascade.
- Downstream consumer: `c-moji0ggh` — Commission C, the surveyor-apparatus substrate, which depends on this cleanup landing first.
- The `setWritExt` API contract is documented on `ClerkApi` in `clerk-apparatus`'s public types — that's the load-bearing primitive.

yields.output:
All four artifacts are written. Here's a summary of the primer pass:

## Summary

**Brief reconstruction.** `plan-show` only returned envelope fields — no brief was attached or accessible from the worktree. I inferred the commission from the codex name (`x018-calibration-baseline-cartograph`) and the recent commit trajectory ending in `b8af5eb cartograph: createVision accepts initial phase + stage` plus the `aff280e docs(architecture): add surveying-cascade design` doc that explicitly names `createCharge`/`createPiece` as the next parity step in §3.6. The inventory flags the inference openly so the patron-anima can override if their reading of the brief differs.

**Inventory.** Mapped the cartograph package, inlined `createVision`'s validation pattern (the shape to mirror) and its 7-test coverage matrix, identified blast radius as single-package (no external `createCharge`/`createPiece` callers), and noted README drift on the file the commission already touches as concurrent doc updates needed.

**Scope (4 items).** S1/S2 extend the API for charge/piece in parity with vision; S3 mirrors the test set; S4 updates README (and backfills missing vision docs from `b8af5eb`).

**Decisions (8).** Pre-filled `selected` on every decision per the attended-primer instructions. Key choices:
- Mirror `{ draft: 'new', active: 'open' }` for both charge and piece.
- Default omitted parameters to `(new, draft)` to keep existing callers zero-touch.
- Leave patron CLI untouched (vision-create precedent).
- Inline-per-method validation rather than extracting a helper.
- Per-type error phrasing rather than reusing "born retired".
- Full test parity (14 new tests).
- Minimal README rework + vision backfill.

**Observations (2).**
- `obs-1`: forward-looking race — `vision-apply` writes `ext['surveyor']` in a separate transaction from `createVision`, so the future surveyor observer will see the create event before the ext slot is set. Lifted as a separate concern because the fix expands the API surface beyond the inferred commission.
- `obs-2`: `vision-apply.STAGE_TO_PHASE` and `cartograph.VISION_INITIAL_STAGE_TO_PHASE` look like they should agree but deliberately don't — a near-bug divergence worth documenting before charge-apply/piece-apply analogues replicate the pattern three-fold.

The existing `createVision({ stage })` parameter — which Commission A relied on for the single-event-per-apply guarantee — keeps its semantic: the cartograph writes the writ at its initial phase, stamps `ext['cartograph']` with the supplied stage, and (if the stage isn't the writ-type's initial state) transitions to it, all inside one `stacks.transaction`.

### 4. Read-side projection

`showVision(id)` / `listVisions(filters)` continue to return `VisionDoc`-shaped values. Internally they:

- read the writ via `clerk.show(id)` (or `clerk.list({ type: 'vision' })`)
- project `writ.ext['cartograph'].stage` and `writ.ext['cartograph'].codex` onto the returned shape
- carry the writ's `createdAt` and `updatedAt` directly

The existing `VisionDoc` / `ChargeDoc` / `PieceDoc` exported types stay as projection shapes (their definitions can be retained verbatim — the only difference is where the data comes from). Filtering on `stage` becomes a JSON-field filter against `writ.ext['cartograph'].stage`; Stacks' query layer supports this.

### 5. Companion-book deletion

`cartograph/visions`, `cartograph/charges`, and `cartograph/pieces` Stacks books are removed entirely. There is no migration of historical companion-doc rows — the cartograph is recently-shipped and nothing in production-style guild data depends on those books. Any test fixtures or integration tests that opened the companion books directly should be updated to read via the typed API.

### 6. Surveying-cascade arch doc updates

The settled architecture for the surveying cascade (`docs/architecture/surveying-cascade.md`) names the companion-doc pattern explicitly in three sections. Update them to reflect the post-cleanup shape:

- **§3.4 — Companion `SurveyDoc` holds envelope metadata only.** SurveyDoc was modelled on the cartograph companion-doc pattern. Rewrite this section so the survey-writ envelope metadata (rigVersion, surveyorId, completedAt — minus targetNodeId, which is already `writ.parentId`, and minus rigName, which is already `writ.type`) lives in `status['surveyor']` / `ext['surveyor']` sub-slots on the survey writ rather than in a `books.surveys` companion book. Note that `completedAt` is redundant with `writ.resolvedAt`. The substrate is the only writer.

- **§3.6 — Substrate watches cartograph CDC.** The current text lists three book-event streams (`book.cartograph.visions.{created,updated}`, `book.cartograph.charges.{created,updated}`, `book.cartograph.pieces.{created,updated}`). Update to a single subscription on the writs book filtered by `writ.type ∈ {vision, charge, piece}`. The single-event-per-apply guarantee discussion stays — it shifts from "wrap createVision + transition in one transaction" to "the cartograph's createX/transitionX primitives are already transactional" with the same outcome (one CDC-significant phase transition per apply).

- **§3.7 — Substrate plugin shape.** The substrate-owned-things list includes "Owns survey writ types and `books.surveys`." Drop `books.surveys` from the list. The substrate now owns the `status['surveyor']` and `ext['surveyor']` slots on survey writs instead. Likewise drop "Stamps SurveyDoc on completion" and replace with "Stamps `status['surveyor']` on completion."

These three sections are the only load-bearing references; minor incidental references elsewhere in the doc may need to be touched up for consistency.

## Out of scope

- **Migrating SurveyDoc itself.** The surveyor-apparatus doesn't exist yet (Commission C in the surveying-cascade design subtree). This commission updates the arch doc to reflect the post-cleanup shape so Commission C is briefed against it, but does not build the substrate.
- **Migrating other companion-doc patterns elsewhere in the framework.** The Astrolabe `PlanDoc` and similar are out of scope; PlanDoc is mostly content (inventory, scope, decisions, spec) and isn't a metadata-only companion.
- **Reworking the `ext` API itself.** `clerk.setWritExt` and the slot-write contract are existing primitives; this commission consumes them, doesn't extend them.
- **Renaming `VisionDoc` / `ChargeDoc` / `PieceDoc` projection types.** They keep their names and shapes — only their backing storage moves.

## References

- Parent design subtree: `c-moji050w` — Cartograph + surveying cascade.
- Downstream consumer: `c-moji0ggh` — Commission C, the surveyor-apparatus substrate, which depends on this cleanup landing first.
- The `setWritExt` API contract is documented on `ClerkApi` in `clerk-apparatus`'s public types — that's the load-bearing primitive.

writ.title: Cartograph: collapse companion docs into `writ.ext['cartograph']`
writ.body:
# Cartograph: collapse companion docs into `writ.ext['cartograph']`

## Intent

The Cartograph apparatus today maintains three companion books — `cartograph/visions`, `cartograph/charges`, `cartograph/pieces` — each holding a `{id, stage, codex, createdAt, updatedAt}` document keyed one-for-one to a writ id. This pattern predates the writ-level `ext` slot. Now that `writ.ext` exists as a sanctioned plugin-keyed metadata slot with a transactional read-modify-write API, those companion books carry pure metadata that can live directly on the writ row.

This commission collapses the three companion books into a single `writ.ext['cartograph'] = { stage, codex }` sub-slot. The cartograph's typed-API surface stays — every existing callable continues to work with the same arguments and return shapes — but the internals shed three Stacks books, three companion-doc shapes, and the dual-write transactional ceremony around them. `createdAt` / `updatedAt` were always duplicates of the writ's own timestamps; they go away.

## Motivation

- **The companion-doc pattern is the pre-`ext` workaround for plugin-owned writ metadata.** Now that `ext` exists as the sanctioned slot, a pure-metadata companion book is exactly what `ext` was added to replace.
- **The substrate work that depends on the cartograph (the surveyor-apparatus, see Commission C in the surveying-cascade design subtree) is best designed against the post-cleanup shape.** Building the substrate's CDC observer, completion-stamping logic, and rig routing on top of the current companion-doc pattern, then reshaping it later, is strictly more work than landing the cleanup first. The substrate's CDC observer becomes a writ-type-filtered subscription (a primitive Stacks already supports cleanly) rather than a per-companion-book subscription.
- **No external behavioral change is intended.** Every consumer (CLI tools, tests, the apply CLI shipped in Commission A) reads the same shape from the same API. The cleanup is internal.

## Non-negotiable decisions

### 1. `ext['cartograph']` is the canonical home for stage and codex

```typescript
interface CartographExt {
  stage: VisionStage | ChargeStage | PieceStage;  // type depends on the writ type
  codex?: string;
}
```

The plugin id is `cartograph`. The slot is plugin-keyed under the established convention; the cartograph apparatus is the only writer.

### 2. The typed-API surface stays stable

`CartographApi` keeps every method it has today:

- `createVision` / `createCharge` / `createPiece`
- `showVision` / `showCharge` / `showPiece`
- `listVisions` / `listCharges` / `listPieces`
- `patchVision` / `patchCharge` / `patchPiece`
- `transitionVision` / `transitionCharge` / `transitionPiece`

Argument shapes (`CreateVisionRequest`, `TransitionChargeRequest`, etc.) and return shapes (`VisionDoc`, `ChargeDoc`, `PieceDoc`) stay. The doc shapes are projections over `writ.ext['cartograph']` rather than rows in a separate book — but from the caller's view, nothing changes.

`createdAt` and `updatedAt` on the projection map to the writ's own timestamps.

### 3. Atomicity for create + transition

`clerk.transition` silently strips `ext` from its body (per the existing Clerk contract). Any operation that touches both `writ.phase` and `writ.ext['cartograph']` MUST wrap the `clerk.transition` and `clerk.setWritExt` calls in a single `stacks.transaction` so the pair is atomic. This applies to `createX` (post + setWritExt) and `transitionX` (transition + setWritExt) alike.

The existing `createVision({ stage })` parameter — which Commission A relied on for the single-event-per-apply guarantee — keeps its semantic: the cartograph writes the writ at its initial phase, stamps `ext['cartograph']` with the supplied stage, and (if the stage isn't the writ-type's initial state) transitions to it, all inside one `stacks.transaction`.

### 4. Read-side projection

`showVision(id)` / `listVisions(filters)` continue to return `VisionDoc`-shaped values. Internally they:

- read the writ via `clerk.show(id)` (or `clerk.list({ type: 'vision' })`)
- project `writ.ext['cartograph'].stage` and `writ.ext['cartograph'].codex` onto the returned shape
- carry the writ's `createdAt` and `updatedAt` directly

The existing `VisionDoc` / `ChargeDoc` / `PieceDoc` exported types stay as projection shapes (their definitions can be retained verbatim — the only difference is where the data comes from). Filtering on `stage` becomes a JSON-field filter against `writ.ext['cartograph'].stage`; Stacks' query layer supports this.

### 5. Companion-book deletion

`cartograph/visions`, `cartograph/charges`, and `cartograph/pieces` Stacks books are removed entirely. There is no migration of historical companion-doc rows — the cartograph is recently-shipped and nothing in production-style guild data depends on those books. Any test fixtures or integration tests that opened the companion books directly should be updated to read via the typed API.

### 6. Surveying-cascade arch doc updates

The settled architecture for the surveying cascade (`docs/architecture/surveying-cascade.md`) names the companion-doc pattern explicitly in three sections. Update them to reflect the post-cleanup shape:

- **§3.4 — Companion `SurveyDoc` holds envelope metadata only.** SurveyDoc was modelled on the cartograph companion-doc pattern. Rewrite this section so the survey-writ envelope metadata (rigVersion, surveyorId, completedAt — minus targetNodeId, which is already `writ.parentId`, and minus rigName, which is already `writ.type`) lives in `status['surveyor']` / `ext['surveyor']` sub-slots on the survey writ rather than in a `books.surveys` companion book. Note that `completedAt` is redundant with `writ.resolvedAt`. The substrate is the only writer.

- **§3.6 — Substrate watches cartograph CDC.** The current text lists three book-event streams (`book.cartograph.visions.{created,updated}`, `book.cartograph.charges.{created,updated}`, `book.cartograph.pieces.{created,updated}`). Update to a single subscription on the writs book filtered by `writ.type ∈ {vision, charge, piece}`. The single-event-per-apply guarantee discussion stays — it shifts from "wrap createVision + transition in one transaction" to "the cartograph's createX/transitionX primitives are already transactional" with the same outcome (one CDC-significant phase transition per apply).

- **§3.7 — Substrate plugin shape.** The substrate-owned-things list includes "Owns survey writ types and `books.surveys`." Drop `books.surveys` from the list. The substrate now owns the `status['surveyor']` and `ext['surveyor']` slots on survey writs instead. Likewise drop "Stamps SurveyDoc on completion" and replace with "Stamps `status['surveyor']` on completion."

These three sections are the only load-bearing references; minor incidental references elsewhere in the doc may need to be touched up for consistency.

## Out of scope

- **Migrating SurveyDoc itself.** The surveyor-apparatus doesn't exist yet (Commission C in the surveying-cascade design subtree). This commission updates the arch doc to reflect the post-cleanup shape so Commission C is briefed against it, but does not build the substrate.
- **Migrating other companion-doc patterns elsewhere in the framework.** The Astrolabe `PlanDoc` and similar are out of scope; PlanDoc is mostly content (inventory, scope, decisions, spec) and isn't a metadata-only companion.
- **Reworking the `ext` API itself.** `clerk.setWritExt` and the slot-write contract are existing primitives; this commission consumes them, doesn't extend them.
- **Renaming `VisionDoc` / `ChargeDoc` / `PieceDoc` projection types.** They keep their names and shapes — only their backing storage moves.

## References

- Parent design subtree: `c-moji050w` — Cartograph + surveying cascade.
- Downstream consumer: `c-moji0ggh` — Commission C, the surveyor-apparatus substrate, which depends on this cleanup landing first.
- The `setWritExt` API contract is documented on `ClerkApi` in `clerk-apparatus`'s public types — that's the load-bearing primitive.

The implementation brief and task manifest have been written to plan `w-mooo5cyd-f8e5538b05a3`.

**Summary of the brief:**

- **Title:** cartograph: createCharge and createPiece parity with createVision
- **Intent:** Extend `CartographApi.createCharge` and `CartographApi.createPiece` to accept optional initial `(phase, stage)` parameters, mirroring the previously-shipped `createVision` extension and satisfying the substrate's single-event-per-apply guarantee.
- **Blast radius:** Single-package (`@shardworks/cartograph-apparatus`); patron CLI tools deliberately untouched; concurrent README updates for all three create methods.
- **All 8 patron-confirmed decisions** are reflected in the Decisions table (mirror-vision mappings, optional-with-defaults, CLI tools untouched, inline-per-method, per-type error phrasing, full-parity test coverage, minimal-plus-vision-backfill README).
- **7 acceptance signals** covering typecheck, tests, single-CDC-event behavior, terminal-stage rejection, default preservation, CLI invariance, and README documentation.
- **Reference Material** inlines the `createVision` validation+resolution block, the `CreateVisionRequest` shape, the stage enums, the test parity table, the CDC-count idiom, the substrate contract from `surveying-cascade.md` §3.6, and the patron CLI precedent — so the implementer has all reasoning self-contained.
- **Task manifest:** 7 sequential tasks (t1–t7), each with executable `verify` commands and outcome-level `done` criteria — types first, methods next (charge then piece), tests, README, then a final verification of CLI/blast-radius invariance.

No gap report was needed — the plan's decisions fully covered the design space.

yields.spec:
# cartograph: createCharge and createPiece parity with createVision

## Intent

Extend `CartographApi.createCharge` and `CartographApi.createPiece` to accept optional initial `(phase, stage)` parameters, so a caller can land a charge or piece directly at `(phase=open, stage=active)` in a single Stacks transaction — and therefore a single CDC `create` event on the `cartograph.charges` / `cartograph.pieces` book. This brings both methods to parity with the previously-shipped `createVision` extension and satisfies the substrate's single-event-per-apply guarantee for all three writ types.

## Rationale

The substrate-readiness contract in `docs/architecture/surveying-cascade.md` §3.6 names `createCharge` and `createPiece` alongside `createVision` as the three create methods that must accept initial stage as a parameter, so the future surveyor-apparatus's CDC observer sees one create event per logical operation rather than a `create` followed by a `transition`. `createVision` already shipped this in commit `b8af5eb`; this commission lands the structurally-identical change for the remaining two writ types so the substrate observer can subscribe to all three books with the same contract. No surveyor-apparatus consumer ships in this commission — the parameters prepare the surface that a later substrate will rely on.

## Scope & Blast Radius

**Single package: `@shardworks/cartograph-apparatus`.** A worktree-wide grep finds no callers of `createCharge` / `createPiece` outside this package; blast radius is confined.

The change touches three concerns within the package:

- **Type surface.** The `CreateChargeRequest` and `CreatePieceRequest` shapes gain optional `phase` and `stage` fields with stage-enum-specific types. The JSDoc on each must enumerate the valid initial pairs and call out the terminal-rejection behavior, mirroring the existing `CreateVisionRequest` doc.
- **Method bodies.** `createCharge` and `createPiece` gain a validation+resolution block — duplicated inline per method, mirroring `createVision` — that maps the optional parameters to a concrete `(phase, stage)` pair, rejects mismatched pairs, rejects terminal initial stages, and defends against an unregistered phase value via the registered writ-type config.
- **Tests.** `cartograph.test.ts` gains the full parity test set for both writ types — default state, explicit `(open, active)`, stage-only resolution, mismatch rejection, terminal-stage rejection, and the load-bearing single-CDC-event assertion that watches `cartograph.charges` / `cartograph.pieces`.

**Concurrent doc updates needed.** The cartograph README's `CartographApi` block currently shows the legacy signatures and additionally has not yet documented the `createVision` `phase`/`stage` fields that landed in `b8af5eb`. The README signature block must be brought current for all three create methods in this commission — the vision backfill closes existing drift on the same surface the brief is editing.

**Patron CLI tools stay untouched.** `tools/charge-create.ts` and `tools/piece-create.ts` are patron-facing wrappers that mirror `tools/vision-create.ts`'s precedent of not exposing the new parameters — patron-facing creation always lands at `(new, draft)` and uses `transition-x` to advance. Do not modify the CLI tools.

**Verification method for the blast radius.** Grep the worktree for `createCharge\(` and `createPiece\(` to confirm the consumer set is exactly: the cartograph package's own tests, the patron CLI tools (which keep their existing signatures), and the cartograph apparatus itself.

## Decisions

| #  | Decision                                                                  | Default                                                                      | Rationale                                                                                                                                                                |
|----|---------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| D1 | Initial `(phase, stage)` pairs for `createCharge`                          | mirror `createVision`: `{ draft: 'new', active: 'open' }`; reject `validated` and `dropped` as initial | Phase machine is identical across all three writ types; substrate guarantee requires `(open, active)` in one tx.                                                          |
| D2 | Initial `(phase, stage)` pairs for `createPiece`                           | mirror `createVision`: `{ draft: 'new', active: 'open' }`; reject `done` and `dropped` as initial      | Symmetric with D1; substrate observer subscribes to all three books with the same contract.                                                                              |
| D3 | Are the parameters optional with defaults, or required?                   | both optional; defaults `(phase=new, stage=draft)`                            | Mirrors `createVision`; preserves zero-touch for the existing patron CLI tools and tests.                                                                                |
| D4 | Patron CLI tools (`charge-create`, `piece-create`) expose the new flags?  | no — leave CLI tools unchanged                                                | `vision-create` precedent: patron-facing creation always lands at default initial state and advances via transition. The new parameters target the future substrate.    |
| D5 | Validation logic shape                                                    | inline per method, duplicated across the three `createX` methods              | Mirrors `createVision`'s prior art; preserves direct readability of each method's contract; per-type error messages stay specific. Three sites stay adjacent for future refactor. |
| D6 | Terminal-initial-stage error message convention                           | per-type phrasing — e.g. "a charge cannot be born terminal" / "a piece cannot be born terminal" | Domain-accurate wording per type is more useful than borrowing vision's "retired" metaphor; tests can match the simpler stem `/cannot be born/` for robustness.            |
| D7 | Test coverage shape                                                       | full parity with `createVision`'s 7 test cases for each of charge and piece (14 new tests total) | The validation block is duplicated per D5 — each duplication is a fresh copy-paste-error opportunity. Full parity catches those at write-time at bounded cost.            |
| D8 | README scope                                                              | minimal-plus-vision-backfill — update the `CartographApi` signature block for all three create methods, including backfilling the `createVision` fields that `b8af5eb` left undocumented | Closes existing vision README drift inline (concurrent doc updates needed pattern) without expanding the README into substrate territory it currently doesn't cover.       |

## Acceptance Signal

1. `pnpm -w typecheck` passes.
2. `pnpm -w test --filter @shardworks/cartograph-apparatus` passes, with the new test cases for charge and piece visible in the test report.
3. The single-CDC-event assertion holds for both `createCharge` and `createPiece` — calling either with `(phase=open, stage=active)` produces exactly one `create` event on the corresponding book and zero `update` events.
4. Calling `createCharge` with a terminal stage (`validated` or `dropped`) throws an error matching `/cannot be born/`; same for `createPiece` with `done` or `dropped`.
5. Calling `createCharge` or `createPiece` with no phase/stage arguments produces a writ at `(phase=new, stage=draft)` — the existing default behavior for all current consumers is preserved.
6. `grep -nE 'createCharge\(|createPiece\(' packages/plugins/cartograph/src/tools/` shows the patron CLI tools still calling without `phase`/`stage` arguments — the CLI surface is unchanged.
7. The cartograph README's `CartographApi` block documents `phase?` and `stage?` on all three create-request types, including `CreateVisionRequest`.

## Reference Material

### `createVision`'s validation+resolution block — the pattern to mirror

**Source:** `packages/plugins/cartograph/src/cartograph.ts` lines 148–151 (constants) and 271–301 (method body). **Role:** the inline-per-method pattern (D5) to replicate twice — once inside `createCharge`, once inside `createPiece`. The default `(phase=new, stage=draft)` falls out of `INITIAL_STAGE` plus the lookup; a caller may supply either or both fields and whatever they supply must agree.

```typescript
// Stage→phase admissibility map (cartograph.ts lines 148–151)
const VISION_INITIAL_STAGE_TO_PHASE: Record<string, WritPhase> = {
  draft: 'new',
  active: 'open',
};

// Inline validation+resolution block (cartograph.ts lines 271–301)
const requestedStage: VisionStage = request.stage ?? INITIAL_STAGE;
const expectedPhase = VISION_INITIAL_STAGE_TO_PHASE[requestedStage];
if (expectedPhase === undefined) {
  throw new Error(
    `[cartograph] createVision: stage "${requestedStage}" is not a valid initial stage. ` +
      `A vision cannot be born retired — allowed initial stages are "draft" and "active". ` +
      `To drive a vision to a terminal state, create it then call transitionVision.`,
  );
}
const requestedPhase: WritPhase = request.phase ?? expectedPhase;
if (requestedPhase !== expectedPhase) {
  throw new Error(
    `[cartograph] createVision: phase "${requestedPhase}" does not pair with stage "${requestedStage}". ` +
      `Allowed initial pairs are (phase=new, stage=draft) and (phase=open, stage=active).`,
  );
}
// Defensive check against the registered writ-type config — catches an
// unregistered or typo'd phase before we write the writ row.
const visionConfig = clerk.getWritTypeConfig('vision');
if (!visionConfig) { throw new Error(/* ... */); }
const requestedState = visionConfig.states.find((s) => s.name === requestedPhase);
if (!requestedState) { throw new Error(/* ... */); }
```

### `CreateVisionRequest` — the type shape to mirror

**Source:** `packages/plugins/cartograph/src/types.ts` lines 187–205. **Role:** the type-side change to mirror in `CreateChargeRequest` and `CreatePieceRequest` — each gains optional `phase?: WritPhase` and a `stage?: <ChargeStage | PieceStage>`. JSDoc on lines 169–186 of the same file enumerates the two valid initial pairs and the terminal-rejection rule; the charge/piece JSDoc must match that level of detail using the type-appropriate stage enum.

```typescript
export interface CreateVisionRequest {
  title: string;
  body: string;
  codex?: string;
  phase?: WritPhase;     // shipped in b8af5eb
  stage?: VisionStage;   // shipped in b8af5eb
}
```

### Stage enums and per-type initial-stage admissibility

**Source:** `packages/plugins/cartograph/src/types.ts`. **Role:** the per-type stage enum the new `stage?` field is typed against. The first two members of each enum are non-terminal (allowed as initial); the second two are terminal-equivalent (rejected as initial).

```typescript
export type VisionStage = 'draft' | 'active' | 'sunset'    | 'cancelled';
export type ChargeStage = 'draft' | 'active' | 'validated' | 'dropped';
export type PieceStage  = 'draft' | 'active' | 'done'      | 'dropped';
```

The initial-stage map for charge and piece mirrors `VISION_INITIAL_STAGE_TO_PHASE` byte-for-byte: `{ draft: 'new', active: 'open' }`. Terminal-stage error messages follow D6 — type-appropriate phrasing, e.g. "a charge cannot be born terminal — initial stages are draft and active" and "a piece cannot be born terminal — initial stages are draft and active". Tests assert against the stem `/cannot be born/`.

### `createVision` test coverage — the test set to mirror

**Source:** `packages/plugins/cartograph/src/cartograph.test.ts`. **Role:** the canonical test set to replicate per writ type (full parity per D7). Each test below has an existing line reference for the vision case.

| Vision test (existing, line)                                 | Charge analogue (write)                                            | Piece analogue (write)                                          |
|--------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------|
| Default state `(new, draft)` — line 229 (vision); already exists at line 399 (charge) and line 488 (piece) | _existing_                                                          | _existing_                                                       |
| Explicit `(open, active)` accepted — line 254                | new                                                                | new                                                              |
| Stage-only `active` resolves phase to `open` — line 266      | new                                                                | new                                                              |
| Phase-only `open` rejected as mismatch (`/does not pair/`) — line 277 | new                                                                | new                                                              |
| Reject terminal initial stage (`/cannot be born/`) — `sunset` line 296, `cancelled` line 311 (vision) | reject `validated` and reject `dropped` (write both)               | reject `done` and reject `dropped` (write both)                  |
| Reject mismatched pair `(new, active)` (`/does not pair/`) — line 326 | new                                                                | new                                                              |
| Single CDC event count — line 342                            | new (watches `cartograph.charges`)                                  | new (watches `cartograph.pieces`)                                |

### Single-CDC-event idiom

**Source:** `packages/plugins/cartograph/src/cartograph.test.ts` lines 342–364. **Role:** the canonical CDC-count idiom to replicate against `cartograph.charges` and `cartograph.pieces` — `fix.stacks.watch<DocType>(...)` registered before the create call, then `assert.equal(createCount, 1)` and `assert.equal(updateCount, 0)` after, for both default-state and direct `(open, active)` creation.

### Substrate contract being satisfied

**Source:** `docs/architecture/surveying-cascade.md` §3.6. **Role:** the contract the change exists to satisfy. _Do not Read._ Verbatim relevant passage:

> Apply CLI and surveyor rigs MUST produce a single CDC event per logical operation, not separate create+transition events. Concretely:
> - `cartograph.createVision` (and `createCharge`, `createPiece`) must accept the initial stage as a parameter and write writ + companion doc + final stage in one Stacks transaction, OR the apply CLI must wrap create + transition in one transaction at its layer.
> - The substrate observer dedupes by writ id within a short window (defensive — should never matter if upstream is transactional, but catches integration bugs cheaply).

### Patron CLI precedent — leave unchanged

**Source:** `packages/plugins/cartograph/src/tools/vision-create.ts`. **Role:** the precedent confirming D4 — patron CLI tools do not expose the new parameters. `charge-create.ts` and `piece-create.ts` are byte-shape clones of the same pattern with `parentId: z.string()` added. _Do not Read._

```typescript
export default tool({
  name: 'vision-create',
  description: 'Create a new top-level vision',
  // ...
  params: {
    title: z.string().describe('Short human-readable title describing the vision'),
    body: z.string().describe('Long-form vision text, stored on the writ body'),
    codex: z.string().optional().describe('Optional target codex'),
  },
  permission: 'write',
  callableBy: ['patron'],
  handler: async (params) => {
    const cartograph = guild().apparatus<CartographApi>('cartograph');
    return cartograph.createVision({
      title: params.title,
      body: params.body,
      ...(params.codex !== undefined ? { codex: params.codex } : {}),
    });
  },
});
```

### Prior-art commit

**Source:** commit `b8af5eb cartograph: createVision accepts initial phase + stage`. **Role:** the immediately-preceding implementation of the same shape against the vision writ type. `git show b8af5eb` returns a diff the implementer can replay structurally against charge and piece. _Do not Read._

## What NOT To Do

- **Do not modify the patron CLI tools.** `tools/charge-create.ts` and `tools/piece-create.ts` keep their existing Zod schemas and continue calling `createCharge` / `createPiece` without `phase` / `stage` arguments (D4).
- **Do not extract a shared validation helper.** D5 chose inline-per-method; the three `createX` methods stay structurally parallel. A future refactor may consolidate, but not in this commission.
- **Do not extract a shared `INITIAL_STAGE_TO_PHASE` constant across the three methods.** Each method gets its own per-type constant (`CHARGE_INITIAL_STAGE_TO_PHASE`, `PIECE_INITIAL_STAGE_TO_PHASE`) even though the maps are byte-identical to `VISION_INITIAL_STAGE_TO_PHASE` — same rationale as D5.
- **Do not reuse the "cannot be born retired" phrase.** D6 chose per-type phrasing; charge and piece use "cannot be born terminal" (or equivalent type-accurate wording). Tests match the stem `/cannot be born/`.
- **Do not extend the README example or add substrate-readiness prose.** D8 chose minimal-plus-vision-backfill — the signature block updates and the missing `createVision` doc, nothing more.

- **Do not write a charge-apply or piece-apply tool.** No surveyor-bound apply tool ships in this commission; the new parameters exist as forward-looking surface area, unconsumed by any concrete code path landed here.
